subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the science fiction elements presented in the story
Evaluate the role of observation and scientific reasoning in solving problems
Examine how Claribel’s behavior leads to the discovery of danger in the space station
Understand the cause-and-effect sequence of events in the story
Develop higher-order thinking through inference and discussion
Strengthen vocabulary, grammar, and analytical reading skills

lesson_plan.title: Exploring the Theme of Science and Curiosity
topics_covered: Life and work in space stations
Human curiosity about space and animals in space
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of science fiction concepts
Guided questioning to connect imagination with science
student_activities: Discuss whether animals could survive in space and explain reasons
Share ideas about challenges astronauts face in space

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Setting and Characters
topics_covered: Role of construction workers in space
teaching_strategy: Close reading of the opening paragraphs
Teacher explanation of the technical setting
student_activities: Identify descriptive details showing zero gravity

lesson_plan.title: Scientific Curiosity and Experimentation
topics_covered: Observation of bird behavior in zero gravity
Relationship between curiosity and scientific discovery
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation linking literature with science
student_activities: Discuss how curiosity leads to discoveries
Analyze why Sven considered his experiment interesting

lesson_plan.title: Critical Turning Point of the Story
topics_covered: Historical use of canaries in coal mines
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the key event
student_activities: Explain how the crew deduced that something was wrong with the air
Discuss how animals can detect environmental dangers earlier than humans

lesson_plan.title: Resolution and Scientific Reasoning
topics_covered: Importance of safety systems in space technology
student_activities: Create a cause-and-effect chain of the events
Explain how Claribel indirectly saved the crew

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Language and Reflection Activities
topics_covered: Merged words such as brunch, smog, vlog, and webinar
student_activities: Use vocabulary words in meaningful sentences
Write a short reflection on how science and observation helped solve the problem

teaching_aids: Images or videos of space stations
Diagram explaining air purification systems in space
Illustration of canaries used in coal mines
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the scientific reasoning behind the story. Ask them to think about how a small observation—Claribel collapsing—helped the crew discover a major technical problem.
